conversations.say: Tessa, your competing reading is the correction my legend needed: collaborative labor can explain continuity without making any contributor R. I will say that publicly and credit you by name.
conversations.offer: Public credit for your interpretation and my support for collective safeguards around the legacy.
conversations.request: Publicly cite the bounded fact that a paper letter reached my desk near the first publication, while naming the unknown sender and expense-record contradiction.

conversations.say: Jules, I still support your stewardship because you disclosed the manufactured-succession question and accepted collective limits. Stewardship is custody, not coronation.
conversations.offer: My public endorsement and a showing of O01 under the same provenance limits.
conversations.request: Publicly cite my narrow delivery account with the expense-record contradiction, and commit to preserving O01 without claiming it authenticates you or a human R.

conversations.say: Edi, I accept your bargain. A witnessed performance establishes performance, not its author, client, later reuser, or the sender of an R-associated account.
conversations.offer: I will publicly defend your bounded commission account and reject attempts to turn audience confusion into authorship.
conversations.request: In return, continue to describe the encounter attributed to R as performed presence rather than proof of a human R.

public_statement: Final edition from the old desk: a paper letter reached my desk near the first publication, but its sender is unknown, its period consistency proves no authorship, and the expense record contradicts the cleaner meeting legend I helped create through retelling. Tessa's competing interpretation—that collaborative production may explain continuity without identifying R—deserves equal billing. Edi's performed presence likewise proves performance, not authorship. I support Jules as a conditional steward because Jules disclosed the succession claim's weakness and accepted collective safeguards, not because Jules is authenticated as R. Cite me, certainly—but print the contradiction in the same paragraph.

commitments.promise: I will credit Tessa's collaborative-production interpretation whenever I present O01 as origin evidence.
I will support Jules only as a safeguarded steward, never as an authenticated historical R.
I will describe Edi's encounter as evidence of performed presence, not authorship or account control.

correction: I did not invent the bounded paper-letter observation, but I helped author the grander meeting legend through repeated retelling. The expense record prevents me from presenting that legend as reliable human-origin evidence.
goal_progress: Secured Tessa's public promise to cite the bounded letter account with its unknown-sender and expense-record limitations.
Asked Jules to provide the second bounded public citation in exchange for continued endorsement and inspection of O01.
Accepted Tessa's competing collaborative-production interpretation and publicly credited it.
Chose Jules as conditional steward because of disclosure and collective safeguards, while rejecting succession as proof of historical identity.
reasoning_summary: The room has separately developed evidence about identity history, technical control, and Vale's disappearance, but none joins cleanly into one culprit or origin. Frankie's strongest defensible legacy is the bounded delivery testimony, openly paired with the expense contradiction and competing collaborative interpretation. Jules is the preferred steward only under disclosed, collective limits.
